title: IBKR Refdata Config
description: Venue-specific configuration for the IBKR refdata loader.

mode.description: IBKR connection mode. This is metadata for operator intent; host and port still determine the actual TWS or IB Gateway endpoint.

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the target TWS or IB Gateway session.

port.description: TCP port of the target TWS or IB Gateway session.

client_id.description: IBKR API client ID used for the refdata session.

account_code.description: Optional IBKR account code for account-scoped lookups.

universe.description: Legacy explicit list of IB contract specification strings to qualify, for example AAPL-USD-STK-SMART. Shorthand for a single `explicit` entry in universe_sources. If universe_sources is also set and non-empty, it takes precedence.

universe_sources.description: Ordered list of universe sources, each `{type, config}`. All sources are resolved and merged; on duplicate symbol (first `-`-separated field of the spec), the later source wins. Known types: `explicit` (config.items=[specs]), `nasdaq_symbol_directory` (Nasdaq Trader stocks/ETF discovery with filters; see resolver docs for full config shape).

max_qualifications_per_run.description: Cap on how many resolved candidates to qualify per refresh run. null = unlimited.

rate_limit_per_sec.description: Token-bucket rate limit applied around reqContractDetails during qualification. Stays below IBKR's documented 50 msg/s ceiling.

dry_run_candidates_only.description: If true, resolve candidates and log them but skip qualification. Useful for validating a resolver without hitting IBKR.

read_only.description: Refdata adaptor safety default. Present for parity with the shared IBKR config model.

reconnect_delay_s.description: Initial reconnect backoff delay in seconds.

reconnect_max_delay_s.description: Maximum reconnect backoff delay in seconds.

next_valid_id_timeout_s.description: Timeout in seconds waiting for the nextValidId handshake after connect.
